short_text: Axar to Bairstow, Bowled!! If it was grip and turn that got the last wicket, this is Axar's classic skidder that's got t
full_text: Axar to Bairstow, Bowled!! If it was grip and turn that got the last wicket, this is Axar's classic skidder that's got this one! Full and just outside off, Bairstow didn't get his feet close to this, fished at it with his hands and played for turn, it landed just outside off and just went on with the arm to hit off-stump. Axar is absolutely delighted! He's got two wickets in two overs now and England are in some trouble. Losing three wickets in the powerplay seldom bodes well.

full_text: Bumrah to Bairstow, no run, Good length delivery just outside off, Bairstow was rooted to his crease as he played at this outside off, snuck along the ground and went under the bat, great take from Pant

short_text: Bumrah to Philip Salt, Bowled!! Both openers are gone now and this is a Bumrah special! A lovely off cutter on off-stump
full_text: Bumrah to Philip Salt, Bowled!! Both openers are gone now and this is a Bumrah special! A lovely off cutter on off-stump, Philip Salt was not expecting the slower one this time, backed away a little and swiped across the line far too early, the ball cut towards leg-stump and hit the top of it. Bumrah is elated and so is the Indian team that surrounds him in celebration. What was looking like a good start for England has suddenly turned into a fantastic start for India.

short_text: Axar to Buttler, Caught by Pant!! Big wicket for India. Length delivery on off-stump, just grips in the surface and turn
full_text: Axar to Buttler, Caught by Pant!! Big wicket for India. Length delivery on off-stump, just grips in the surface and turns, not characteristic for Axar as he usually skids it through, Buttler went for a reverse-sweep straightaway and it lobbed up after taking the toe end, Pant only had to turn around and take a step or two before completing an easy catch. Superb bowling change and it yields results instantly.
